name: Ayesha Rahman
content: BICTA Hackathon was a game-changer for me. Building a working health-tech prototype in 48 hours and getting direct feedback from industry CTOs completely changed how I approach software engineering.

name: Kamrul Islam
content: The mentorship I received during the finals was incredible. We didn't win the grand prize, but the connections we made landed our entire team internships at top tech firms in Dhaka.

name: Samia Hossain
content: Our startup actually started as a BICTA hackathon project. The initial seed money and validation we got from the judges gave us the confidence to turn our project into a real company.

name: Fahim Ahmed
content: I've participated in many programming contests, but the scale and organization of the National ICT Programming Festival is unmatched. The energy on the competition floor is simply electric.

name: Nafisa Chowdhury
content: What impressed me most was the diversity of tracks. As someone focused on Machine Learning, I was thrilled to find a dedicated track with highly specialized mentors who truly understood our technical hurdles.

name: Tariq Mahmud
content: Beyond just coding, BICTA emphasizes product viability and UX. Learning how to pitch our technical solution to business leaders was the most valuable part of the entire festival.
